questions.hint: The first number is the horizontal move and the second is the vertical move. ⟨−3, 2⟩ means 3 left and 2 up.
questions.explanation: ⟨−3, 2⟩ moves 3 units left (negative horizontal) and 2 units up, so the tip lands at (−3, 2). The first number controls left/right movement and the second controls up/down.

questions.prompt: A vector is shown. What vector is it?
questions.hint: Follow the arrow from the origin. Count how far it moves right or left, then how far up or down.
questions.explanation: The arrow moves 2 units right and 3 units down from the origin, so it is the vector ⟨2, −3⟩. A downward move makes the second number negative.

questions.prompt: Draw a vector with magnitude 4 and direction 90°.
questions.hint: Direction 90° points straight up. A magnitude of 4 means the arrow is 4 units long, so move 4 units up.
questions.explanation: 90° points straight up, and magnitude 4 means a length of 4. So the vector is ⟨0, 4⟩: 0 horizontal and 4 up. Direction tells you which way it points and magnitude tells you how long it is.
